places.name: South Haven South Pierhead Light
places.address: South Pier, Mouth of Black River, South Haven, MI 49090
places.notes: Historic lighthouse lit in 1872, still operational. Not open for public tours due to liability, but 'sneak peek' lower level access offered during HarborFest and Labor Day weekend. Sits at the west end of the South Pier at the mouth of the Black River — great photo op and walk. The Historical Association museum (Hartman School Building, 355 Hubbard St) is open Tue–Wed 9am–3pm and Sun 11am–4pm (Memorial Day through Labor Day), featuring exhibits on South Haven history, beach, cottages, and lighthouse.

places.name: Ludington Ghost Tour
places.notes: Evening walking ghost tour through downtown Ludington exploring eerie legends including the haunted Stearns Hotel. Guided tours with expert storytellers blending local history and paranormal lore. Rated 4.8/5 stars. Extended tour option available. Great for teens and adults. Book in advance online.

places.name: Grand Mere State Park
places.notes: Magnificent sand dunes, deep blowouts, and 0.5 miles of Lake Michigan shoreline accessed via two trails. Two inland lakes (South and Middle) behind the dunes popular for bluegill fishing. Great for hikers and runners. Track chair available to borrow at no cost for accessibility. Pets allowed on shoreline on 6-foot leash. Hunting occurs on Dune Trail during designated seasons — use caution. Pavilion with picnic tables and grills near main parking lot. No alcohol March 1–Sept 30 without permit. Snowshoeing allowed in winter.
places.cost: Recreation Passport required for vehicle entry (Michigan State Park pass)
